summary: Completed comprehensive testing of NutriScan AI Spanish nutrition label scanner. Backend authentication system working perfectly, but found critical frontend auth state management issue preventing UI updates after successful login/registration.

ui_bugs.issue: After successful registration/login, user menu does not appear and login button remains visible
ui_bugs.details: Token is successfully stored in localStorage but React auth state (user, setUser) is not updating, causing UI to remain in unauthenticated state

integration_issues.issue: Auth state not syncing with API responses
integration_issues.details: API calls succeed (200 status) and token is stored, but useAuth hook doesn't update user state

passed_tests: Backend API /api/ root endpoint working
Backend user registration API creates account successfully
Backend user login API returns valid token and user data
Backend get current user API (/api/auth/me) working
Backend profile update API working with allergies and conditions
Backend logout API working
Backend analysis API working with Gemini 3 Flash integration
Backend personalized alerts generation based on user profile
Frontend landing page loads correctly with dark theme
Frontend navbar displays NutriScan logo and login button
Frontend scan button visible and clickable
Frontend auth modal opens correctly
Frontend registration form has all 3 steps working
Frontend registration step 1: credentials (name, email, password)
Frontend registration step 2: profile data (weight, height, sex)
Frontend registration step 3: allergies and conditions selection
Frontend camera modal opens with proper UI
Frontend camera input has capture='environment' attribute
Frontend footer contains all required legal links
API calls from frontend to backend are successful (confirmed via backend logs)
Token storage in localStorage working correctly

success_percentage.backend: 100% - All authentication and analysis endpoints working perfectly
success_percentage.frontend: 85% - UI components working but critical auth state management bug

action_item_for_main_agent: Fix frontend auth state management in useAuth hook. The issue is that after successful API calls (register/login), the React state (user, setUser) is not being updated properly, causing the UI to remain in unauthenticated state despite having a valid token in localStorage. Check the useAuth hook's response handling in the register() and login() functions.
